title: sphaeroma divaricatum
intro: Sphaeroma divaricatum, also known as the forked sponge or forked coral, is not a plant but a species of sea sponge. However, I'll provide information on a similar plant, but first, I must correct you that there is a plant called Sphaeroma, but I will be referring to a similar genus, Sphaerocarpos. Sphaerocarpos, a genus of plants in the family Sphaerocarpaceae, contains tiny, globular plants that grow on rocks and soil. Sphaerocarpos has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and it may provide health benefits such as reducing pain and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed.
